name: Cranberry Ribbon Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups finely crushed chocolate cookies (15 cookies)
3 packages (8 oz each) cream cheese, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease a 10-inch springform pan.
3. In a small bowl, mix the crust ingredients and press into the bottom of the greased pan. Set aside.
4. In a medium saucepan, combine 1 cup sugar and cornstarch; stir until smooth. Add cranberries and cranberry juice. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly (about 5 minutes). Continue cooking for an additional 2 minutes. Remove from heat and process in a food processor or blender until smooth. Set aside to cool.
5. In a large bowl, beat the cheesecake filling until light and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in sour cream and orange peel.
6. Pour half of the filling into the crust-lined pan. Drizzle with half of the cranberry sauce. Cover and refrigerate remaining filling and sauce for topping.
7. Carefully pour the remaining filling over the cranberry sauce in the pan.
8. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 60 to 70 minutes, or until the center is set. To minimize cracking, place a shallow pan half full of hot water on the lower rack during baking.
9. Cool to room temperature. Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight.
10. Just before serving, run a knife around the edge of the pan. Carefully remove the sides of the pan. Spread the reserved cranberry sauce over the cheesecake. Serve chilled.
16 servings
High Altitude - Above 3500 Feet: No change.
